prerequisites: A dbt Cloud account, and your numeric dbt Cloud account ID (the account_id config field) used in the Administrative API path /api/v2/accounts/{account_id}/.

A dbt Cloud API token to use as the api_key_2 secret: either a Service Token (recommended for service/production access) or a Personal Access Token (PAT). Legacy user API keys are deprecated and no longer work.

Sufficient permissions on the token to read the resources you want to extract. Service tokens can be granted permission sets such as Job Viewer, Read-only, or Metadata Only, scoped to all projects or specific projects; a PAT inherits the permissions of the user who created it.

authMethods.name: dbt Cloud API token (Service Token or Personal Access Token)
authMethods.summary: The api_key_2 secret holds a dbt Cloud token. Both Service Tokens and Personal Access Tokens are accepted and are sent in the HTTP Authorization header as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'. Use a Service Token for service/production access (it belongs to the account and supports least-privilege permission sets) or a Personal Access Token, which inherits the creating user's permissions. User API keys are deprecated.

setupSteps.title: Find your dbt Cloud account ID
setupSteps.body: Determine your numeric dbt Cloud account ID. This is the account identifier used as the {account_id} path parameter in the Administrative API v2 (requests go to /api/v2/accounts/{account_id}/...). Enter this value in the account_id config field.

setupSteps.title: Create a Service Token (recommended for service access)
setupSteps.body: In dbt Cloud, click your account name in the left side menu and select Account settings, then click Service Tokens in the left sidebar and click + New Token. Assign the permission sets you need (for read-only extraction, sets like Job Viewer, Read-only, or Metadata Only) and scope them to all projects or specific projects. Copy the generated token immediately, because it cannot be viewed again.

setupSteps.title: Or create a Personal Access Token (PAT)
setupSteps.body: Alternatively, in Account Settings expand API tokens and click Personal tokens, then Create personal access token. Give it a descriptive name and click Save. Copy the token before closing the window, as it will not be shown again. The PAT inherits the permissions of the user who created it.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the Service Token or Personal Access Token as the api_key_2 secret. The connector sends it as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' against the dbt Cloud Administrative API v2.

sources.title: dbt Cloud API reference (Administrative API v2)
